data.text: MRI PELVIS W&W/O CONTRASTClinical Indication: Elevated PSA and microhematuria.Technique: Multi-parametric prostate MRI using 9 cc of Gadavistintravenous contrast.  Comparison: None.Biopsy: None.PSA:  7.5 ng/mL on 4/11/2022PSA density:  0.42 ng/mL/ccThe prostate gland measures 4.3 x 2.9 x 3.0 cm (18 cc). No focal suspicious T2 signal within the transitional zone. Scattered mildly heterogeneous T2 signal within the peripheralzone bilaterally without focal restricted diffusion, likelyprostatitis/scarring.In the left peripheral zone from the apex almost to the base from2-5 o'clock there is a 1.1 x 0.5 x 1.9 cm PI-RADS 5 lesion. Thisbroad-based interface with the capsule and adjacent tissue issuspicious for extraprostatic extension and subtle focal bulgetoward the left neurovascular bundle in the apex is suggested(series 6-image 14).There are is T2 hypointensity (series 6 - images 10/15), ADC maphypointensity and high b-value DWI (series 8/102 - images 10/14)and there is focal and early enhancement.  Seminal vesicles are symmetric. Recto- prostatic fat and fascial planes are intact.No focal bladder wall thickening. The urinary bladder is markedlydistended even after several attempts at urination.No enlarged pelvic lymph nodes.Imaged bowel and rectum without wall thickening. No free fluid. No suspicious bone marrow signal or enhancement.
